content.question: The economic sustainability threshold—the minimum school size or village population below which per-pupil costs become budgetarily unmanageable—is not precisely defined in current Hungarian policy or research; this threshold varies by property condition, staffing flexibility, and central-subsidy design.
content.reduction_path: Detailed micro-level financial audits of a stratified sample of small schools (50–150 pupils) in different regions, with transparent accounting of building maintenance, teacher-salary spreading, and realistic alternative arrangements (mergers, satellite campuses, multi-grade classes), would establish the true sustainability boundary.
